title: My Cat
hint: Short CVC words and sight words.

content.text: I have a cat. The cat is fat.

content.passage: I have a cat. The cat is fat.

questions.question: What is this passage mostly about?

title: A Big Dog
hint: Short CVC words and sight words.

content.text: The dog is big. He can run to me.

content.passage: The dog is big. He can run to me.

questions.question: What is this passage mostly about?

title: In the Sun
hint: Focus on short u and o sounds.

content.text: It is hot. The sun is up.

content.passage: It is hot. The sun is up.

questions.question: What is this passage mostly about?

title: The Red Bug
hint: Focus on color red and short u sounds.

content.text: I see a red bug. The bug can hop.

content.passage: I see a red bug. The bug can hop.

questions.question: What is this passage mostly about?

content.text: My dad is sad. He has no cap.

content.passage: My dad is sad. He has no cap.

questions.question: What is this passage mostly about?

title: Play in the Mud
hint: Adjectives like brown and happy, and blends like spin.

content.text: The brown pig is in the mud. He is happy. He can jump and spin.

content.passage: The brown pig is in the mud. He is happy. He can jump and spin.

questions.question: What is this passage mostly about?

title: The Green Frog
hint: Look for green, frog, and the number ten.

content.text: A green frog is on the log. He can hop fast. I see ten frogs.

content.passage: A green frog is on the log. He can hop fast. I see ten frogs.

questions.question: What is this passage mostly about?

content.text: I have a red hat. It fits my big head. I wear it to the park.

content.passage: I have a red hat. It fits my big head. I wear it to the park.

questions.question: What is this passage mostly about?

content.text: Look at the little frog. It can swim in the pond. It is green and fast.

content.passage: Look at the little frog. It can swim in the pond. It is green and fast.

questions.question: What is this passage mostly about?

title: Ten Red Birds
hint: Check number ten, color red, and blend fly.

content.text: I see ten red birds in the tree. They sing a happy song. They fly away.

content.passage: I see ten red birds in the tree. They sing a happy song. They fly away.

questions.question: What is this passage mostly about?

title: At the Fish Shop
hint: Focus on digraph sh (she, fish, shop) and past tense went, saw, liked.

content.text: She went to the fish shop. She saw a big fish. The fish was red and blue. She liked it a lot.

content.passage: She went to the fish shop. She saw a big fish. The fish was red and blue. She liked it a lot.

questions.question: What is this passage mostly about?

title: The Playful Chick
hint: Digraph ch (chick), vowel team (seed, sweet), and past tense had, ate, ran, was.

content.text: The little yellow chick had a small nest. He ate a sweet seed. Then he ran to his mom. He was warm.

content.passage: The little yellow chick had a small nest. He ate a sweet seed. Then he ran to his mom. He was warm.

questions.question: What is this passage mostly about?

title: A Day at the Lake
hint: Long a silent-e words: lake, gate, tame, cake.

content.text: We went to the big lake. We saw a white gate. A tame duck ate cake near the gate. It was a fun day.

title: Comprehension: A Day at the Lake

content.passage: We went to the big lake. We saw a white gate. A tame duck ate cake near the gate. It was a fun day.

questions.question: What is this passage mostly about?

title: Where is the Toy?
hint: Question words (where) and digraph th (the, here).

content.text: Where is my red toy? It is not on the bed. Did it fall under the rug? Yes, here it is!

content.passage: Where is my red toy? It is not on the bed. Did it fall under the rug? Yes, here it is!

questions.question: What is this passage mostly about?

title: The Cold Wind
hint: Past tense blew, sat, drank, slept.

content.text: The cold wind blew all night. We sat by the warm fire. We drank hot milk and slept well in our beds.

content.passage: The cold wind blew all night. We sat by the warm fire. We drank hot milk and slept well in our beds.

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
Choose the correct plural.
She ___ to school every day.
We felt ___ when we won the game.
Hot is the opposite of ___.
Which sentence ends correctly?

title: Common Nouns

questions.question: Which word is a person, place, or thing?

hint: Nouns name people, places, or things.

title: Adjectives

questions.question: Which word describes the noun?

hint: Adjectives tell what kind or how many.

questions.question: Choose the best pronoun: ___ went to the store.
I wanted to play, ___ it was raining.
Choose the correct article: ___ umbrella.

title: Commas in a List

questions.question: Which list uses commas correctly?

hint: Separate items in a list with commas.

questions.question: Yesterday we ___ to the lake.
Which word means almost the same as big?
Which word is like fast?
Sad is the opposite of ___.
The puppy was very ___. It wagged its tail and licked my hand.
Which word is stronger than said?
Unhappy means not happy. Unpack means ___.
Bat can mean an animal or a sports tool. In "The bat flew at night," bat means ___.
Rewrite means to write again. Reread means ___.

content.passage: The yellow bus stops at our corner. Kids line up with their backpacks. The driver smiles and says good morning.

questions.question: What color is the bus?
What do kids carry?

content.passage: We have a gold fish in a bowl. We feed it flakes twice a day. The fish swims in circles.

questions.question: What kind of pet?
What do they feed it?

content.passage: We count red and blue blocks at math time. There are ten blocks in all. We stack them into a tower.

questions.question: How many blocks in all?
What colors are the blocks?

content.passage: In art we use crayons and paper. Maya draws a house with a red door. She shares her picture with the class.

questions.question: What does Maya draw?
What color is the door?

content.passage: At recess we play tag and hopscotch. When the bell rings, we line up quietly. Then we walk back to class.

questions.question: What games do they play?
What do they do when the bell rings?

content.sentence: I smiled because it was fun.
Each book tells a different story.
